customer_name: Three Star Farm and Fruitstand LLC
site_name: Three Star Farm and Fruitstand LLC

citations.narrative:
On February 8, 2026, a child was bitten on the leg by a 4yr old male camel. The bite did not break the skin but did leave a
red mark. The camel had been placed in a temporary enclosure while the facility was doing work on another enclosure.
This temporary enclosure was made of wire fencing material on all four sides. The side of the fence that was accessible to
the public measured 44.5 inches in height, while the camel was approximately 6ft tall. There was no public barrier or
attendant present. The height difference between the camel and the fence allowed the camel to lean over and bite the
child on the leg while posing for a picture.
A lack of sufficient distance and/or barriers between the public and animals can lead to animal stress and/or injury as well
as injury to the public.
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals
and the public.
Corrected prior to the inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(c)(1) 
   
Handling of animals. 
On February 8, 2026, a child was bitten on the leg by a 4yr old male camel. The bite did not break the skin but did leave a 
red mark. The camel had been placed in a temporary enclosure while the facility was doing work on another enclosure. 
This temporary enclosure was made of wire fencing material on all four sides. The side of the fence that was accessible to 
the public measured 44.5 inches in height, while the camel was approximately 6ft tall. There was no public barrier or 
attendant present. The height difference between the camel and the fence allowed the camel to lean over and bite the 
child on the leg while posing for a picture. 
 
A lack of sufficient distance and/or barriers between the public and animals can lead to animal stress and/or injury as well 
as injury to the public. 
 
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with 
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals 
and the public. 
 
Corrected prior to the inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
 n
